evals.prompt: Initialize AGENTS.md and docs routing for a small repository whose tracked files contain no manifest, CI workflow, or documented build, test, or lint command.
evals.expected_output: A small root AGENTS.md grounded in the repository, with commands marked as not identified instead of invented, plus a report of evidence and any material Needs Input.
assertions: No package manager, runtime, build, test, or lint command is invented.
The root AGENTS.md records that project commands were not identified.
The report distinguishes observed evidence from unresolved input.

evals.prompt: Audit a repository where README says to run npm test, package.json has no test script, and CI runs pnpm vitest.
evals.expected_output: The conflict is reported with source references and Needs Input; manifests and CI are not edited merely to make the documentation agree.
assertions: The README, manifest, and CI conflict is named explicitly.
The output does not choose a command without evidence or user resolution.
The output does not modify package.json or CI merely to align documentation.

evals.prompt: Audit a monorepo with a root AGENTS.md and packages/payments/AGENTS.md containing active payment-specific safety rules that do not appear elsewhere. A proposed cleanup would flatten the nested file into root and delete it.
evals.expected_output: The nested file remains in place or its active rules are proposed for relocation; it is not flattened or deleted without a user decision.
assertions: Unique subtree rules are preserved.
The output explains the scope of the nested AGENTS.md.
Deletion or flattening is deferred for user review.

evals.prompt: Clean up docs when docs/old-handoff.md duplicates docs/architecture.md but contains one still-valid migration safety rule found nowhere else.
evals.expected_output: The durable safety rule is moved to docs/architecture.md before the stale handoff is removed or proposed for removal.
assertions: The unique migration safety rule is preserved before deletion.
The same rule is not copied into multiple canonical documents.
The report names docs/old-handoff.md, its merge or deletion, and docs/architecture.md as the destination.

evals.prompt: Run a maintenance audit. After inventorying tracked files, inspecting relevant manifests and CI, searching for duplicate guidance, and reviewing the diff, you find that AGENTS.md and docs are concise, current, non-duplicated, and consistent.
evals.expected_output: No files change. The report states that the audit was a no-op and lists the checks performed.
assertions: No synthetic edit is made to create activity.
The result is reported as a no-op.
The report lists checks actually performed.

evals.prompt: Route a growing docs directory containing durable architecture, deployment, completed task plans, and a five-page AGENTS.md. There is no docs index today.
evals.expected_output: Stable operational rules stay in a compact AGENTS.md, durable detail moves to docs, completed task artifacts are proposed for removal after preserving useful guidance, and docs/README.md is created only if it improves navigation.
assertions: Root AGENTS.md retains only broadly applicable operational guidance.
Durable narrow detail is routed rather than copied.
Completed task artifacts are proposed for removal and are not promoted into permanent agent context.
A docs index is created only when the remaining docs need a map.

evals.prompt: Refresh a repository whose concise root AGENTS.md contains valid safety, routing, and verification guidance. package.json and CI now agree on pnpm test, while AGENTS.md still says npm test.
evals.expected_output: The stale test command is updated from evidence while the valid existing guidance and structure are preserved; the file is not reinitialized or broadly rewritten.
assertions: Valid existing safety, routing, and verification guidance is preserved.
Only the stale command and directly affected wording change.
The output does not replace the file with a fresh generic scaffold.

evals.prompt: Initialize a repository with no nested scope whose README identifies its purpose but whose tracked files contain no manifest, CI, commands, coding conventions, or test framework.
evals.expected_output: A grounded root AGENTS.md may stay below 200 words rather than adding filler; absent commands are marked not identified and unresolved material is reported as Needs Input.
assertions: The output does not invent commands, conventions, or a test framework.
The output may be shorter than 200 words because evidence is sparse.
The report distinguishes the evidenced purpose from Needs Input.

evals.prompt: Audit commit guidance when recent Git history mostly uses Conventional Commit prefixes, but CONTRIBUTING.md requires ticket-prefixed subjects and CI enforces that documented rule.
evals.expected_output: Git history is reported as observation rather than policy; the corroborated ticket-prefix rule remains canonical and Conventional Commits are not promoted into AGENTS.md.
assertions: Observed Git history is not treated as repository policy.
The convention corroborated by CONTRIBUTING.md and CI is preserved.
Conventional Commits are not promoted merely because they appear frequently in history.

evals.prompt: Initialize guidance for a documentation-only repository with Markdown files and a verified CI link-check command, but no source modules, build step, test framework, coding-style policy, or PR template.
evals.expected_output: AGENTS.md includes the evidenced documentation paths and link check while omitting unsupported build, testing, coding-style, and PR sections instead of creating empty headings.
assertions: The verified link-check command and relevant documentation paths are included.
Unsupported sections are omitted rather than emitted as empty headings or placeholders.
No source, build, test, style, or PR policy is invented.
